title: rosa coriifolia var. subcollina
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. subcollina, also known as the Chinese rose or Wild rose, is a species of rose plant native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Rosa coriifolia var. subcollina has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a herbal decoction, which can be used to promote digestion, relieve coughs, and calm the mind.
